Rows: A related question: People often use the nonparametric Mann-Whitney test for this kind of data. Since there are only five possible values, there will be lots of tied ranks. The Mann-Whitney test adjusts for tied ranks, but does this adjustment work when there are a huge number of ties?
You may be interested in this recent article published in PARE, *Five-Point Likert Items: t test versus Mann-Whitney-Wilcoxon*, http://j.mp/biLWrA.

gt_sql: SELECT T2.Id, T1.Text FROM comments AS T1 INNER JOIN posts AS T2 ON T1.PostId = T2.Id WHERE T2.Title = 'Group differences on a five point Likert item'

Rows: A related question: People often use the nonparametric Mann-Whitney test for this kind of data. Since there are only five possible values, there will be lots of tied ranks. The Mann-Whitney test adjusts for tied ranks, but does this adjustment work when there are a huge number of ties?
You may be interested in this recent article published in PARE, *Five-Point Likert Items: t test versus Mann-Whitney-Wilcoxon*, http://j.mp/biLWrA.

pred_sql:
SELECT p.Id, c.Text 
FROM posts p
JOIN comments c ON p.Id = c.PostId
WHERE TRIM(p.Title) = 'Group differences on a five point Likert item';
question: What is the post ID and the comments commented in the post titled by "Group differences on a five point Likert item"?
